label: Explanation
title: ingest.js Purpose & Logic
summary: Asked for an explanation of ingest.js — its purpose, logic, and flowchart of how it works

label: Error
title: canvas npm install Failure (node-gyp / Missing C++ Toolset)
summary: npm install failed on the canvas package because Visual Studio is missing the Desktop development with C++ workload. Fixed by switching to @napi-rs/canvas

label: Error
title: ffprobe Command Failed — Empty JSON stdout
summary: ingestVideo() threw an error because -v quiet hid the real ffprobe message. Root cause: myvideo.mp4 did not exist in the project root

label: Error
title: ffprobe — No Such File or Directory
summary: After removing -v quiet, confirmed the file myvideo.mp4 simply did not exist at the path being passed to ffprobe

label: Changes
title: ingest.js — Auto-prepend input/ Folder to File Path
summary: Changed ingestVideo() to accept just a filename and automatically prepend the input/ folder using path.join()

label: Changes
title: prompt_log_viewer.html — Fix Zero Counts (Embed Data Instead of Fetch)
summary: Viewer showed 0 counts because fetch() is blocked on file:// protocol. Fixed by embedding LOG_DATA directly in the HTML script tag

label: Changes
title: Canvas Import — Switch from canvas to @napi-rs/canvas
summary: Updated require('canvas') to require('@napi-rs/canvas') across the project to use the pre-built Windows-compatible package

label: Explanation
title: scenes.js — Purpose, Logic & Flowchart
summary: Asked for an explanation of scenes.js similar to the ingest.js breakdown — covering purpose, FFmpeg scene filter, stderr parsing, threshold, and output format

label: Explanation
title: scenes.js — Re-explained in Plain Text with Flowchart
summary: Same explanation as #8 but in plain Claude format with inline SVG flowchart only — no HTML file created

label: Error
title: scenes.js — detectScenes() Returns Only [{start:0, end:Infinity}]
summary: Two causes: (1) /dev/null doesn't exist on Windows — fixed by changing to NUL. (2) Default threshold 0.3 too strict for footage with gradual changes — lowering to 0.1 resolved it. Advised stepping up slowly to find the right value.

label: Explanation
title: Orchestrator Prompt Additions — Points 3 & 4 (Auto-Suggest + Doodle Input)
summary: Explained what to add to the Claude orchestrator system prompt to support auto-suggestion of additional editing techniques (Point 3) and doodle/stickman theme input wiring (Point 4)

label: Explanation
title: pacing.js — Purpose, Logic & Flowchart
summary: Explained pacing.js: reads scene timestamps from detectScenes(), computes avgCutSeconds, variance (std deviation), and assigns a style label (fast-cut / medium / cinematic) to enable editing pace cloning from sample videos

label: Error
title: orchestrate.js — Gemini Skipping doodleInstructions, autoSuggestions, captionStyle
summary: Gemini returned incomplete JSON — doodleInstructions and autoSuggestions were missing, captionStyle was empty. Fixed by adding responseSchema with SchemaType definitions and a required array to force all fields in every response.

label: Changes
title: orchestrate.js — Switch to Google Gemini 1.5 Flash (Free, No Payment Needed)
summary: Replaced OpenAI with Google Gemini 1.5 Flash as the orchestrator LLM. Free tier via Google AI Studio, no credit card required. Used @google/generative-ai SDK with responseMimeType: application/json for reliable JSON output. Prompt unchanged.

label: Explanation
title: orchestrate.js — How to Test generateEditPlan()
summary: Explained how to test orchestrate.js: node one-liner with dummy scenes, real Week 2 style profile, theme/effects/doodleTheme inputs. Covered expected JSON output shape and common errors (missing openai package, bad API key, JSON parse error, config.json path)

label: Changes
title: orchestrate.js — Use OpenAI (GPT-4o) Instead of Anthropic Temporarily
summary: Rewrote orchestrate.js to use OpenAI SDK (gpt-4o) as a temporary replacement while Anthropic API key is unavailable. Config key changed to openaiKey. Full prompt retained including Auto-Suggestion Rule and Doodle Instructions. Easy swap back to Anthropic when key is ready.

label: Explanation
title: orchestrate.js — Where to Add Auto-Suggestion Rule in the Prompt
summary: Explained that the Auto-Suggestion Rule belongs inside the prompt template string in generateEditPlan(), after the style profile block and before the JSON structure example. Also explained why it goes there — Claude has no memory between calls so all instructions must be in the single message.

label: Error
title: analyseSamples — avgCutSeconds: null (Infinity from single-scene detection)
summary: avgCutSeconds was null because detectScenes() returned only [{start:0,end:Infinity}] with default threshold 0.3. JSON.stringify(Infinity) produces null. Fixed by lowering threshold to 0.1 in pacing.js and adding isFinite(d) guard to duration filter

label: Error
title: colorgrade.py — File Not Found + analyse vs analyze Spelling Mismatch
summary: Two issues: (1) colorgrade.py had not been created on disk yet. (2) analyser.js hardcoded 'src/analyse/' but user's folder is named 'src/analyze/' (American spelling). Fixed by creating the file and updating the path in analyzer.js

label: Error
title: analyser.js — Missing src/utils.js (execP not found)
summary: analyser.js requires execP from '../utils' but the file didn't exist. Fixed by creating src/utils.js with a promisified wrapper around child_process.exec

label: Explanation
title: Week 2 Milestone — How to Test Style Analyser (analyseSamples)
summary: Explained how to test Week 2: prerequisites (pip install opencv-python, sample video in input/), test node one-liner, expected JSON output, multi-sample usage, and common errors (python path, cv2 not found, JSON parse error)

label: Explanation
title: colorgrade.py — Purpose, Logic & Flowchart
summary: Explained colorgrade.py: samples 30 frames from a video, converts each to LAB colour space, computes brightness (L channel) and warmth (B channel − 128), then recommends a LUT file (warm_cinematic / cool_clean / neutral_grade) for FFmpeg to apply to the main video
